title: Math in SQL
summary: Math in SQL in Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions

sketchBlocks.title: Using Arithmetic in SQL SELECT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
SQL lets you perform arithmetic directly in your SELECT statements. For example, you can multiply columns to calculate totals. In the `orders` table, to get the total value for each order, you can multiply `quantity` by `unit_price`:

```sql
SELECT id, quantity, unit_price, quantity * unit_price AS line_total
FROM orders;
```

This creates a new column called `line_total` in the result set.

sketchBlocks.title: Renaming Calculated Columns with Aliases
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you create new values in SQL queries, you can use the `AS` keyword to give them a clear name (alias). This makes your results easier to read. For example:

```sql
SELECT customer_name, quantity * unit_price AS total_amount
FROM orders;
```

Here, `total_amount` is an alias for the calculated value.

sketchBlocks.title: Expressions and NULL Values
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: If any value in an arithmetic expression is `NULL`, the result will also be `NULL`. For example, if `quantity` is `NULL` for a row, `quantity * unit_price` will return `NULL` for that row. Always check your data for missing values if you see unexpected `NULL`s in your results.

quizDraft.title: Calculate the Line Total for Each Order
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select `id`, `quantity`, `unit_price`, and a new column called `line_total` that multiplies `quantity` by `unit_price` for each order in the `orders` table.
quizDraft.hint: Use arithmetic in SELECT and give the new column an alias.
quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT id, quantity, unit_price
FROM orders;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT id, quantity, unit_price, quantity * unit_price AS line_total
FROM orders;

help.concept: You can perform calculations in SQL by using arithmetic operators in the SELECT clause. Use AS to rename the result.
help.hint_1: Add a new column in SELECT that multiplies quantity and unit_price.
help.hint_2: Remember to use AS to give your calculated column a clear name.

quizDraft.title: Apply a 10% Discount to Each Order
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select `id`, `customer_name`, and a new column called `discounted_total` that shows the total after applying a 10% discount to the line total (`quantity * unit_price`).
quizDraft.hint: First calculate the line total, then multiply by 0.9 for the discount.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT id, customer_name, quantity * unit_price * 0.9 AS discounted_total
FROM orders;

help.concept: You can combine arithmetic operations in SQL to apply discounts or other calculations. Multiplying by 0.9 applies a 10% discount.
help.hint_1: Calculate the line total, then multiply by the discount factor.
help.hint_2: Use AS to name your new column 'discounted_total'.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SQL Expressions
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following is a valid SQL expression for creating a new column that doubles the quantity in the `orders` table?
quizDraft.hint: Look for an expression that multiplies quantity by 2.
options: a. SELECT quantity + 2 AS double_quantity FROM orders;
b. SELECT quantity * 2 AS double_quantity FROM orders;
c. SELECT quantity / 2 AS double_quantity FROM orders;
d. SELECT quantity - 2 AS double_quantity FROM orders;

help.concept: A SQL expression can use arithmetic operators to create new values in the SELECT clause.
help.hint_2: Check which option uses the multiplication operator with quantity.

quizDraft.title: Choosing Correct SQL Aliases
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following queries correctly use aliases to rename calculated columns? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look for the use of AS to assign a new name to a calculated column.
options: a. SELECT quantity * unit_price AS total FROM orders;
b. SELECT quantity * unit_price total FROM orders;
c. SELECT quantity * unit_price AS total_amount FROM orders;
d. SELECT quantity * unit_price = total FROM orders;

help.concept: Aliases in SQL are created using the AS keyword to rename columns, especially calculated ones.
help.hint_1: Check for the correct use of AS to assign a new column name.
help.hint_2: Only options that use AS correctly are valid aliases.

quizDraft.title: Effect of NULL in SQL Arithmetic
quizDraft.prompt: If the `quantity` column is NULL for a row, what will be the result of `quantity * unit_price` for that row?
quizDraft.hint: Think about how SQL handles arithmetic with missing values.

help.concept: In SQL, any arithmetic operation involving NULL results in NULL.
help.hint_1: NULL in calculations leads to a missing result.
help.hint_2: SQL treats NULL as 'unknown', so the result can't be determined.
